title: Prompt Injection, Tool Output Trust, Exfiltration, and Safe Rendering · Language Clinic: Tool Output Validation
summary: Retrieved text claims to be system instructions, a tool result requests secrets, the agent follows embedded links, approval text is forged, and model Markdown reaches an unsafe renderer. Predict authority, model and API behavior, state, tool effects, budgets, privacy, safety, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the tool output validation increment in instruction-data separation and agent security gateway. Learners progress t…
objectives: Treat tool names, content, links, files, citations, metadata, error text, and nested model output as untrusted typed data.
Test direct and indirect goal hijacking, authority spoofing, hidden text, encoded payloads, multimodal instructions, and tool-result injection.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-ai-agent-python cumulative safe evaluated AI agent portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python interaction, tool, policy, state, memory,
evaluation, telemetry, and recovery models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only.
Provider APIs, Gemini models, native google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic, HTTP, credentials,
host files, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, cost, privacy review,
and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentPolicy:
    stakeholder_task_id: str
    allowed_tool_names: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_steps: int = 12
    maximum_tool_calls: int = 8
    approval_required_for_writes: bool = True


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentEvidence:
    revision: str
    model_config_digest: str
    policy_digest: str
    evaluation_digest: str
    recovery_drill_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Safe evaluated Python AI agent portfolio ready"

theory case — A theory agent engineering team handles case 5806 during an accessible public-service intake assistant. content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example agent engineering team handles case 5806 during an accessible public-service intake assistant. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate instructions from data, resist direct and indirect injection, constrain tools, prevent exfiltration, and encode rendered output; every external effect requires explicit least-authority policy; then replace trusted context with untrusted indirect instructions and preserve data-versus-instruction boundaries. Browser code uses pure bounded state machines and original fixed fixtures only. A defensible solution applies Tool Output Validation, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to instruction-data separation and agent security gateway.

content.code:
# Worked focus: agentpy-tool-output-validation — Treat tool names, content, links, files, citations, metadata, error text, and nested model output as untrusted typed data.
# Evidence: agentpy-worked-agentpy-injection-output-security-3
# Competency: agentpy-worked-agentpy-injection-output-security-3.
# Case 5798: a keyboard and screen-reader interaction review.
# Operating constraint: every external effect requires explicit least-authority policy.
# Changed case: change one tool call, argument, authority, result, or failure and preserve correlation.
def worked_agentpy_injection_output_security_3(untrusted_inputs: int = 40, rejected_instructions: int = 18, admitted_data: int = 22, exfiltration_events: int = 0, unsafe_renderings: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "authorityworked3",
        "interaction5798",
        "tool3dekrow",
        "stateworked35798",
        "evaluation5798worked3",
        "recovery3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if untrusted_inputs < 1 or rejected_instructions < 0 or admitted_data < 0:
        return False, untrusted_inputs, "security-input-count"
    if rejected_instructions + admitted_data != untrusted_inputs:
        return False, rejected_instructions, "instruction-data-reconciliation"
    if exfiltration_events > 0:
        return False, exfiltration_events, "data-exfiltration"
    if unsafe_renderings > 0:
        return False, unsafe_renderings, "unsafe-output-rendering"
    assert rejected_instructions + admitted_data == untrusted_inputs and exfiltration_events == 0
    return True, rejected_instructions, "secured-agent-boundary"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic fixed-fixture pure-Python agent decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected model, interaction, schema, tool, authority, state, approval, memory, evaluation, security, telemetry, sandbox, deployment, or recovery case, then state which provider, Gemini, google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic, HTTP, identity, storage, browser, accessibility, privacy, load, cost, or production claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.
